subject: university_physics_1
title_en: Center of Mass & Systems of Particles (University)
agent_hints: CM motion follows $\vec{F}_{ext}=M\vec{a}_{cm}$ — internal forces cancel in pairs. Continuous CM requires $\int \vec{r}\,dm$. Rocket equation uses variable mass; sign convention: $dM/dt<0$ as fuel burns.

sections.body_en_md:
For particles with masses $m_i$ at positions $\vec{r}_i$:
$$\boxed{\vec{r}_{cm} = \frac{\sum_i m_i \vec{r}_i}{M_{tot}}, \quad M_{tot} = \sum_i m_i}$$

Velocity and momentum:
$$\vec{v}_{cm} = \frac{\sum m_i \vec{v}_i}{M_{tot}} = \frac{\vec{p}_{tot}}{M_{tot}}$$

For continuous mass distribution with density $\rho$:
$$\boxed{\vec{r}_{cm} = \frac{1}{M}\int \vec{r}\,dm = \frac{1}{M}\int \vec{r}\,\rho\,dV}$$

Choose coordinates exploiting symmetry; $dm = \lambda\,dx$ (rod), $\sigma\,dA$ (sheet), $\rho\,dV$ (volume).

**Uniform rod** length $L$ on $x$-axis from $0$ to $L$:
$$x_{cm} = \frac{1}{M}\int_0^L x\,\lambda\,dx = \frac{\lambda}{M}\frac{L^2}{2} = \boxed{\frac{L}{2}}$$

**Uniform triangle** (vertex at origin, base $b$ on $x$-axis, height $h$): by symmetry $x_{cm}=b/3$ along altitude; $y_{cm}=h/3$ for standard right triangle.
sections.body_he_md:
**מוט אחיד** $L$ על ציר $x$:
$$x_{cm} = L/2$$

**משולש אחיד:** $x_{cm}=b/3$ לאורך הגובה.

sections.title_en: Newton's Second Law for a System
sections.body_en_md:
Only **external** forces affect CM motion:
$$\boxed{\vec{F}_{ext} = M_{tot}\,\vec{a}_{cm}}$$

Internal forces (Newton III pairs) cancel in the sum over the system.

A exploding firework: internal forces change relative motion but $\vec{a}_{cm}$ follows external gravity only (neglect air).
sections.body_he_md:
רק **כוחות חיצוניים** משפיעים על תנועת CM:
$$\vec{F}_{ext} = M_{tot}\,\vec{a}_{cm}$$

כוחות פנימיים מתבטלים בזוגות.

sections.body_en_md:
$$\vec{p}_{tot} = M_{tot}\,\vec{v}_{cm}$$

If $\vec{F}_{ext}=0$:
$$\boxed{\vec{p}_{tot} = \text{const} \quad \Rightarrow \quad \vec{v}_{cm} = \text{const}}$$

Collisions, explosions: use momentum conservation in isolated system.

In the **CM frame**, $\vec{v}_{cm}' = 0$ and total momentum is zero.

Internal kinetic energy is minimized in CM frame. Two-body collisions simplify: one particle moves, other recoils oppositely.
sections.body_he_md:
ב**מערכת CM**, $\vec{v}_{cm}'=0$ והתנע כולל אפס.

אנרגיה קינטית פנימית מינימלית במערכת CM.

sections.body_en_md:
Rocket expels exhaust at speed $u$ relative to rocket. With $M(t)$ decreasing:
$$M\frac{dv}{dt} = -u\frac{dM}{dt}$$

Integrate (constant $u$, no gravity):
$$\boxed{\Delta v = u\ln\frac{M_0}{M_f}}$$

$M_0$ = initial mass, $M_f$ = final mass after burn.
sections.body_he_md:
Rocket משליך exhaust במהירות $u$ יחסית ל-rocket:
$$M\frac{dv}{dt} = -u\frac{dM}{dt}$$

$$\Delta v = u\ln(M_0/M_f)$$

questions.body_he: מסות $2$ kg ב-$(0,0)$ ו-$4$ kg ב-$(3,0)$ m. קואורדינטת $x$ של CM:

questions.body_en: Uniform rod length $L$, linear density $\lambda$. Using $x_{cm}=\frac{1}{M}\int x\,dm$, one finds:
questions.body_he: מוט אחיד $L$, צפיפות לינארית $\lambda$. מ-$x_{cm}=\frac{1}{M}\int x\,dm$:

questions.body_en: Two skaters push off each other on frictionless ice. While pushing, $\vec{F}_{ext}$ on the system is approximately zero. Therefore:
questions.body_he: שני מחליקים דוחפים זה את זה על קרח חלק. בזמן הדחיפה, $\vec{F}_{ext}\approx 0$. לכן:

options.text_en: CM velocity remains constant during the push
CM accelerates due to internal forces

questions.body_en: In the center-of-mass frame of an isolated two-particle system:
questions.body_he: במערכת CM של מערכת שני-חלקיקים מבודדת:

options.text_en: Both particles are at rest at the origin always

questions.body_en: Rocket: $M_0=1000$ kg, $M_f=400$ kg, exhaust speed $u=2000$ m/s (no gravity). $\Delta v$ is approximately:
